contentDescriptions.description: Understand how language is used to create tone, mood and atmosphere in a range of literary and informational texts.
Identify and explain how vocabulary and grammatical choices are used to express shades of meaning, feeling and nuance.
Analyse how authors create and develop characters, settings and themes in literary texts from a range of cultural contexts.
Compare texts from different cultural contexts, discussing how context influences meaning and interpretation.
Plan, draft, revise, edit and publish imaginative, informative and persuasive texts for a range of purposes and audiences.
Analyse and evaluate information and ideas, developing arguments supported by evidence.
Analyse how the structure and features of texts reflect the purpose, audience and context of the text.
Explain how rhetorical devices, including irony, hyperbole and allusion, are used to influence readers and viewers.
Identify and explain how authors use narrative techniques and literary devices to engage readers and develop meaning.
Compare texts within and across cultures, identifying how different perspectives influence the representation of events and people.
Compose and edit persuasive, analytical and creative texts using appropriate language structures for different purposes.
Select and use different strategies for reading, viewing and listening, adjusting them to suit the purpose and complexity of the text.
Explain how language choices, including vocabulary, tone and structural features, construct different representations of individuals, groups and events.
Analyse the use of rhetorical and language devices in persuasive texts and explain their intended effects on specific audiences.
Interpret and evaluate representations of people, places, events and ideas in literary texts.
Analyse and explain the ways that authors have developed compelling and complex characters and settings.
Create sustained, complex analytical and persuasive responses with evidence from texts to support arguments.
Compose, edit and refine texts that effectively communicate ideas and arguments for particular audiences using appropriate conventions.
Analyse how language and structural choices shape meaning and influence the interpretation of texts.
Explain how vocabulary, grammatical patterns and text structures are used to express sophisticated ideas and arguments.
Evaluate the ways that language and text structures are used to represent the concerns of particular cultural groups and time periods.
Interpret and synthesise information and ideas from a range of complex texts, and discuss the ways they relate to real-world contexts.
Create, compose and edit sustained analytical, persuasive and creative texts that demonstrate sophisticated understanding of language and literary devices.
Engage with challenging and complex ideas across a range of texts and contexts, evaluating arguments and forming considered positions.
